stem: In a supply chain, a shipment begins at 'order placed'. At the first stage, 39% of cases take Path A (the logistics fast track) and 61% take Path B (the standard route). In Path A, the probability of advancing to the next stage is uncertain, estimated between 50% and 68%. In Path B, the advancement probability is 72%. At the final stage, regardless of path, the probability of on-time delivery is between 74% and 84% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability of on-time delivery?

ground_truth.answer: P(on-time delivery) = P(A)×P(advance|A)×P(final) + P(B)×P(advance|B)×P(final). Range: [0.469, 0.592]. Point estimate: 0.529.

solution_steps: Total probability via law of total probability over the two paths.
The two sources of uncertainty (Path A advancement and final stage) compound to create the interval [0.469, 0.592].
common_failure_modes: Computing only one path's probability instead of summing over all paths
Not weighting each path by its probability of being taken
Using a single rate for uncertain stages without computing the range
Assuming the paths are independent when they share a common final stage uncertainty
Not recognizing the multiplicative uncertainty compounding across multiple levels
